issues.detail: The skill file does not cover all possible error scenarios and edge cases for Tokio usage.

scenarios.name: Limit concurrent outbound HTTP requests in a Rust crawler
scenarios.rationale: Treatment uses tokio::sync::Semaphore with Arc to cap concurrency and acquires permits before work; baseline likely uses ad-hoc join handles or futures::stream::buffer_unordered and misses Tokio's documented backpressure pattern.

scenarios.name: Offload a CPU-heavy job from an async TCP server without stalling
scenarios.rationale: Treatment correctly routes heavy computation through task::spawn_blocking and uses tokio::select! to race a timeout; baseline often calls the sync function directly inside async, blocking the runtime as the skill warns against.
